perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Any M ember of the SAMED Board, Executive, and/or Staff who has a personal interest, directly or indirectly, in any contract, transaction, proposed contract, or proposed transaction, under consideration of the Board of Directors or of a C ommittee created by it shall be deemed as being in a conflict of interest, where: a) Contract is defined as any written or verbal agreement between two or more parties for the doing or not doing of something specified which is related to the matter at hand. b) Transaction is defined a s an exchange of money, services, goods, position, or favours are exchanged for money, services, goods, position or favours. c) Interest is defined as any instance where any contract or transaction, proposed contract or proposed transaction could be to the b enefit or detriment of themselves, family, partners, roommates/housemates, other organizations to which they are currently affiliated, that do not pertain to their job description or mandate.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Any M ember of the SAMED Board, Executive, and/or Staff who has a personal interest, directly or indirectly, in any contract, transaction, proposed contract, or proposed transaction, under consideration of the Board of Directors or of a C ommittee created by it shall be deemed as being in a conflict of interest, where: a) Contract is defined as any agreement between two or more parties related to the matter at hand.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific requirement for the contract to involve 'the doing or not doing of something specified' and removing the detailed definitions of 'Transaction' and 'Interest,' the modified text makes the definition of 'conflict of interest' less clear and enforceable. This creates an in-text contradiction because while the agreement establishes that conflicts of interest must be avoided, the reduced and altered definition of its components doesn't specify the bounds of what could trigger such definition, creating uncertainty for situations where the agreement calls for the Conflict of Interest clauses to apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 A conflict of interest arises when there is a relationship which exists which could result in the Board or Committee member being perceived as being biased either for or against the topic of discussion .
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the scope of 'conflict of interest' by explicitly detailing 'Contract,' 'Transaction,' and 'Interest.' By removing these specific definitions and altering the definition of 'Contract' to a more generic one, the changed text weakens the overall definition of 'conflict of interest'. The contradicted text defines a 'conflict of interest' as a situation where bias is perceived. The original text provided specific examples of what could cause a conflict of interest, and the changed text makes the definition vague, leading to uncertainty as to whether a perceived bias would be seen as a conflict of interest. This vagueness makes it less clear when section 5.1 applies which is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.5 In the event of a conflict of interest, the interested party shall: a) Prior to any vote on the issue, decl are the con flict of interest to the Chair or Board as a whole. b) Refrain from voting in relation to the matter. c) Withdraw from the meeting when the matter is discussed if requested to do so by a simple majority of the members present and voting at the meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 In the event of a conflict of interest, the interested party shall: a) Declare the con flict of interest to the Chair or Board as a whole. b) Consider refraining from voting in relation to the matter.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the mandatory 'Refrain from voting' and 'Withdraw from the meeting' clauses and replacing it with 'Consider refraining from voting,' the agreement now gives conflicted parties discretion in how they act, contradicting the original intent to strictly manage conflicts of interest. This undermines the clause 5.1 which states that a conflict of interest exists when a board member is 'perceived as being biased either for or against the topic of discussion'. With the modified clause, the perception of bias remains, but the actions taken to mitigate it are no longer mandatory and it is up to the interested party, which can be deemed as a biased agent in this situation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 A conflict of interest arises when there is a relationship which exists which could result in the Board or Committee member being perceived as being biased either for or against the topic of discussion .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 5.5 by removing the mandatory requirements for an interested party to 'Refrain from voting' and 'Withdraw from the meeting' when a conflict of interest exists. This directly contradicts section 5.1, which states that a conflict of interest arises when a board member is 'perceived as being biased'. By making the actions to mitigate the conflict discretionary rather than mandatory, the changed text allows for a situation where a perceived bias exists (as defined in 5.1) but the interested party is not required to abstain from voting or withdraw, thus undermining the intent of preventing biased decision-making. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition, where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with obligations in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Breaches 6.1 A breach of this Confidentiality and Conflict of Interest Agreement may result in action being taken against the member (up to and including removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee). 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances (provided that consid eration of the removal of a Board member shall be on thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember. After thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember, the Board, may remove the person from the Board through the applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Breaches 6.1 A breach of this Confidentiality and Conflict of Interest Agreement may result in action being taken against the member. 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause '(up to and including removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee)' from section 6.1 and removing the 30 day notice period and reference to 'applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution' from section 6.2, the modified text creates ambiguity about the possible consequences of a breach and the procedures for enforcing them. This omission is an in-text contradiction because the contract discusses the steps that need to be taken against a member that made a breach, but without specifying the parameters needed to take such actions, those steps cannot be taken. Without the removal clause, the steps to be taken are ambiguous, reducing the enforcement of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 A breach of this Confidentiality and Conflict of Interest Agreement may result in action being taken against the member (up to and including removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee).
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific actions that can be taken against a member for breach of contract (removal from Board and/or SAMED Committee) and removes the stipulation of 30 days notice. The contradicted text, which is also the original text, specifies that action may be taken up to and including removal from the Board. The omission in the changed text directly contradicts the original and contradicted text by making the steps that can be taken ambiguous and unenforcable, therefore a reasonable reader would immediately recognize the sections as incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Additional action 8.1 Each party to this agreement shall execute and deliver such other documents and do such other acts and things as may be necessary or desirable to give effect to the terms and provisions of this agreement. This may include the return and/or distraction of documents, information, files, emails and the like that came to be in his/her possession during his/her tenure as a Board - and/or Committee member, upon resignation or removal from such a position.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Additional action 8.1 Each party to this agreement shall execute and deliver such other documents and do such other acts and things as may be necessary or desirable to give effect to the terms and provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'This may include the return and/or distraction of documents, information, files, emails and the like that came to be in his/her possession during his/her tenure as a Board - and/or Committee member, upon resignation or removal from such a position.', the changed text removes a specific example of 'additional action' that parties may need to take. It creates uncertainty because if there is a return and/or distraction of documents, the agreement doesn't specify under which cases it should be done, and the parameters needed to take those actions, which creates a contradiction because now the additional actions is not clear, and cannot be taken properly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Each party to this agreement shall execute and deliver such other documents and do such other acts and things as may be necessary or desirable to give effect to the terms and provisions of this agreement. This may include the return and/or distraction of documents, information, files, emails and the like that came to be in his/her possession during his/her tenure as a Board - and/or Committee member, upon resignation or removal from such a position.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a specific example of 'additional action' relating to the return or destruction of documents upon resignation or removal. The changed text removes this example. The contradicted text mirrors the original text. Thus, the changed text creates an omission, as defined in the instructions, by removing an example that was previously included. This omission makes the clause 'additional actions' unclear, creating a contradiction because it's not clear under what circumstances this applies or what parameters should be followed.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Amendments 9.1 No amendment, interpretation or waiver of any of the provisions of this agreement shall be effective unless reduced to writing and signed by both the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Amendments 9.1 No amendment of any of the provisions of this agreement shall be effective unless reduced to writing and signed by both the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the words 'interpretation or waiver', the modified text now only specifies amendments must be in writing, while interpretations or waivers of existing clauses are left undefined. This is an in-text contradiction because the original clause states the need for 'interpretation' and now, in the actual agreement, the interpretation of it is undefined and can lead to different meanings depending on how the person interprets. This creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 This agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties with respect to the subject matter of this agreement and supersedes all prior agreements between the parties, whether written or oral, with respect to the subject matter of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no amendment, interpretation, or waiver is effective unless in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text removes 'interpretation or waiver', meaning interpretations or waivers are not required to be in writing. This contradicts section 10.1, which states the agreement contains the entire agreement, because now unwritten interpretations or waivers could be argued as part of the agreement, conflicting with the intention of section 10.1, making it a strong in-text contradiction. The explanation provided supports this.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Undertaking Respecting Confidentiality and Con flicts of Interest Whereas , and based on the above rules, from time to time, arising out of the relationship between the undersigned and the Board and/or any Committee of SAMED, the undersigned shall come into possession or have knowledge of certain matters, information and things which are confidential, i.e. “confidential information and s/he may be in situations of conflict as outlined in the aforementioned provisions, therefore the person declares as follows: I have read the above undertakings and certify that I fully understand the nature and effect of these undertakings, and acknowledge receipt of a copy thereof. I further agree to be bound by the rules of confidentiality and conflict of interest as set out above. I also understand the need for these requirements and I believe I can meet the requirements as set out above. Signed at on For Board / Committee Member: Full names and surname of Signature For SAMED: Full names and surname Signature
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Undertaking Respecting Confidentiality and Conflicts of Interest Whereas, arising out of the relationship between the undersigned and the Board and/or any Committee of SAMED, the undersigned may come into possession or have knowledge of certain matters, information and things which may be considered confidential. The person declares as follows: I agree to the general principles of confidentiality and conflict of interest. Signed at on For Board / Committee Member: Full names and surname of Signature For SAMED: Full names and surname Signature
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrases referencing 'the above rules,' 'confidential information,' 'situations of conflict as outlined in the aforementioned provisions,' 'I have read the above undertakings and certify that I fully understand the nature and effect of these undertakings, and acknowledge receipt of a copy thereof', 'I further agree to be bound by the rules of confidentiality and conflict of interest as set out above. I also understand the need for these requirements and I believe I can meet the requirements as set out above', the modified text weakens the commitment and understanding of the agreement. Instead of an active agreement, it has now been reduced to only an 'agreement to the general principles.' This creates an in-text contradiction because the entire agreement is based around the need for confidential information to be secured, but this clause, which is the signature sign, it is said to be only a 'general principle'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 The purpose of this agreement is to ensure that confidential matters brought before the Members of the Board of SAMED or any of its committees , are not disclosed until disclosure is properly authorized. SAMED Board and Committee members are in positions of trust towards SAMED as a vol untary membership organization, and such trust may require that elected and appointed persons act with the highest integrity and keep certain information confidential, and avoid conflicts of interest.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the confidentiality and conflict of interest agreement by removing explicit references to specific rules and understandings. The original text emphasizes a clear understanding and commitment to defined rules, while the changed text reduces it to an agreement to 'general principles.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes the importance of trust, integrity, and the need to keep specific information confidential and avoid conflicts of interest, implying a much stronger and more defined obligation than a mere agreement to 'general principles'. The changed text creates ambiguity where the contradicted text expects specificity and a strong commitment to confidentiality.
